'''唐崎神社'''（からさきじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]にある[[神社]]。
[[琵琶湖]]西岸に位置し、[[近江八景]]'''「唐崎の夜雨」'''で知られる。

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */ とりあえず玄松子さんにリンク
revision.text:
[[画像:Biwako new year 2004.jpg|thumbnail|200px|唐崎神社から琵琶湖の初日の出（2004年）を望む]]
'''唐崎神社'''（からさきじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]にある[[神社]]。
[[琵琶湖]]西岸に位置し、[[近江八景]]「唐崎の夜雨」で知られる。
境内は滋賀県指定史跡になっている。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/oumi/karasaki_title.htm 唐崎神社]（玄松子）
==関連項目== *[[神社一覧]] *[[滋賀県指定文化財一覧#史跡名勝天然記念物]]
[[Category:滋賀県の神社|からさきしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=唐崎神社| 所在地=滋賀県大津市唐崎1-7-1| 祭神=女別當命| 社格=日吉大社摂社| 例祭=[[4月28日]]・[[10月28日]]| 神事= 御手洗祭（[[7月28日]]・[[7月29日|29日]]）}}
[[画像:Biwako new year 2004.jpg|thumbnail|200px|唐崎神社から琵琶湖の初日の出（2004年）を望む]]
'''唐崎神社'''（からさきじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]にある[[神社]]。[[日吉大社]]の[[摂社]]の一つである。
[[琵琶湖]]西岸に位置する。境内は滋賀県指定史跡になっている。
日吉大社の社伝によれば、[[舒明天皇]]6年（[[633年]]）、琴御館宇志丸宿禰がこの地に居住し「唐崎」と名附けたという。祭神の女別當命は宇志丸宿禰の妻である。当社は[[持統天皇]]の時代に創建されたと伝えられる。かつては「女別当社」と呼ばれ、婦人病に霊験ありとして広く信仰を集めた。
境内には、宇志丸宿禰が植えたのに始まるとされる「唐崎の松」がある。境内から琵琶湖を背景に唐崎の松を描いた[[歌川広重]]の「唐崎の夜雨」で知られており、[[近江八景]]に選ばれている。宇志丸宿禰が植えた初代の松は天正9年（1581年）に大風で倒れ、現在の松は天正19年（1591年）に植えられた2代目である。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/oumi/karasaki_title.htm 唐崎神社]（玄松子）
==関連項目== *[[神社一覧]] *[[滋賀県指定文化財一覧#史跡名勝天然記念物]]
[[Category:滋賀県の神社|からさきしんしや]] [[Category:摂末社|からさきしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{otheruses|大津市の唐崎神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 唐崎神社 |画像=[[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[大津市]][[唐崎 (大津市)|唐崎]]1-7-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 女別当命 |社格 = 日吉大社摂社 |創建 = 持統天皇11年（697年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = [[4月28日]]・[[10月28日]] |神事 = 御手洗祭（[[7月28日]]・[[7月29日|29日]]） }}
'''唐崎神社'''（からさきじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]にある[[神社]]。[[日吉大社]]の[[摂社]]の一つである。[[琵琶湖]]西岸に位置し、境内は滋賀県指定史跡になっている。
== 祭神 == '''女別当命'''（わけすきひめのみこと）を祀る。
== 由緒 == 日吉大社の社伝によれば、[[舒明天皇]]6年（[[633年]]）、琴御館宇志丸宿禰がこの地に居住し「唐崎」と名附けたといい、祭神はその宇志丸宿禰の妻とされる。当社は[[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]）に創建されたと伝えられ、かつては「女別当社」と呼ばれ、婦人病に霊験ありとして広く信仰を集めた。
境内には、宇志丸宿禰が植えたのに始まるとされる「唐崎の松」がある。境内から琵琶湖を背景に唐崎の松を描いた[[歌川広重]]の「唐崎の夜雨」で知られており、[[近江八景]]に選ばれている。宇志丸宿禰が植えた初代の松は[[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）に大風で倒れ、天正19年（[[1591年]]）、[[大津城]]主だった[[新庄直頼]]の弟・[[新庄直忠]]が2代目の松を植えた。2代目も[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）に枯れ、現在のものは3代目である。
== 外部リンク == *[http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/ 唐崎神社] - 日吉大社
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からさきしんしや}} [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:日吉神社]] [[Category:琵琶湖]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:滋賀県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: 「平安時代には「七瀬の祓」の一所として京の人々や公家の姫等がお祓いを受けにやってきている」等の文章の追加と校正。
revision.text:
{{otheruses|大津市の唐崎神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 唐崎神社 |画像=[[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[大津市]][[唐崎 (大津市)|唐崎]]1-7-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 女別当命 |社格 = 日吉大社摂社 |創建 = [[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = 春季大祭（[[4月28日]]）・秋季大祭（[[10月28日]]） |神事 = 御手洗祭（[[7月28日]]・[[7月29日|29日]]） }}
'''唐崎神社'''（からさきじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]にある[[神社]]。[[日吉大社]]の[[摂社]]の一つである。[[琵琶湖]]西岸に位置し、境内は滋賀県指定史跡になっている。
== 祭神 == '''女別当命'''（わけすきひめのみこと）を祀る。
== 由緒 == 日吉大社の社伝によれば、[[舒明天皇]]6年（[[633年]]）、琴御館宇志丸宿禰がこの地に居住し「唐崎」と名附けたといい、祭神はその宇志丸宿禰の妻とされる。当社は[[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]）に創建されたと伝えられ、かつては「女別当社」と呼ばれ、婦人病に霊験ありとして広く信仰を集めた。[[平安時代]]には「七瀬の祓」の一所として[[京]]の人々や[[公家]]の姫等がお祓いを受けにやってきている。
境内には、宇志丸宿禰が植えたのに始まるとされる「唐崎の松」がある。境内から琵琶湖を背景に唐崎の松を描いた[[歌川広重]]の「唐崎の夜雨」で知られており、[[近江八景]]に選ばれている。宇志丸宿禰が植えた初代の松は[[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）に大風で倒れ、天正19年（[[1591年]]）、[[大津城]]主だった[[新庄直頼]]の弟・[[新庄直忠]]が2代目の松を植えた。2代目も[[1921年]]（[[大正]]10年）に枯れ、現在のものは3代目である。[[金沢市]]の[[兼六園]]にある唐崎の松はこの地から分けられたものである。

== 文化財 == === 滋賀県指定文化財 === * 名勝 ** 唐崎（唐崎神社境内） - 1998年（平成10年）6月19日指定<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf 県指定史跡・名勝・天然記念物]}}（滋賀県ホームページ）。</ref>。
=== 大津市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 唐崎 - 1981年（昭和56年）1月16日指定（史跡・名勝）<ref>境内説明板。</ref>。

== 由緒 == 日吉大社の社伝によれば、[[舒明天皇]]6年（[[633年]]）、琴御館宇志丸宿禰がこの地に居住し「唐崎」と名附けたといい、祭神はその宇志丸宿禰の妻とされる。当社は[[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]）に創建されたと伝えられ、かつては「女別当社」と呼ばれ、婦人病に霊験ありとして広く信仰を集めた。[[平安時代]]には「七瀬の祓」の一所として[[京]]の人々や[[公家]]の姫等がお祓いを受けにやってきている。

== 祭神 == '''女別当命'''（わけすきひめのみこと）を祀る。
== 由緒 == 日吉大社の社伝(『日吉社神道秘密記』)によれば、[[舒明天皇]]6年（[[633年]]）、{{Ruby|琴御館宇志丸宿禰|ことのみたちうしまる}}がこの地に居住し「唐崎」と名附けたといい、祭神はその宇志丸宿禰の妻とされる。当社は[[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]）に創建されたと伝えられ、かつては「女別当社」と呼ばれ、婦人病に霊験ありとして広く信仰を集めた。[[平安時代]]には「七瀬の祓」の一所として[[京]]の人々や[[公家]]の姫等がお祓いを受けにやってきている。
境内には、宇志丸宿禰が植えたのに始まるとされる「唐崎の松」がある。境内から琵琶湖を背景に唐崎の松を描いた[[歌川広重]]の「唐崎の夜雨」で知られており、[[近江八景]]に選ばれている。宇志丸宿禰が植えた初代の松は[[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）に大風で倒れ、天正19年（[[1591年]]）、[[大津城]]主だった[[新庄直頼]]の弟・[[新庄直忠]]が2代目の松を植えた。2代目も[[1921年]]（[[大正]]10年）に枯れ、現在のものは3代目である。[[金沢市]]の[[兼六園]]にある唐崎の松はこの地から分けられたものである。

=== 大津市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 唐崎（唐崎神社境内地） - 1981年（昭和56年）1月16日指定（史跡・名勝）<ref>昭和56年1月16日大津市教育委員会告示第3号。</ref><ref>境内説明板。</ref>。

古くから、和歌や俳句に取り上げられている。 {{Quotation|唐崎の 松は扇の 要にて こぎ行く船は 墨絵なりけり|紀貫之（古今和歌集）}} {{Quotation|唐崎の 松は花より 朧にて|松尾芭蕉}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|大津市の唐崎神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 唐崎神社 |画像=[[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[大津市]][[唐崎 (大津市)|唐崎]]1-7-1 |位置 = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 女別当命 |社格 = 日吉大社摂社 |創建 = [[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = 春季大祭（[[4月28日]]）・秋季大祭（[[10月28日]]） |神事 = 御手洗祭（[[7月28日]]・[[7月29日|29日]]） }} '''唐崎神社'''（からさきじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]にある[[神社]]。[[日吉大社]]の[[摂社]]の一つである。[[琵琶湖]]西岸に位置し、境内は滋賀県指定名勝に指定されている。
== 祭神 == '''女別当命'''（わけすきひめのみこと）を祀る。
== 由緒 == 日吉大社の社伝(『日吉社神道秘密記』)によれば、[[舒明天皇]]6年（[[633年]]）、{{Ruby|琴御館宇志丸宿禰|ことのみたちうしまる}}がこの地に居住し「唐崎」と名附けたといい、祭神はその宇志丸宿禰の妻とされる。当社は[[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]）に創建されたと伝えられ、かつては「女別当社」と呼ばれ、婦人病に霊験ありとして広く信仰を集めた。[[平安時代]]には「七瀬の祓」の一所として[[京都|京]]の人々や[[公家]]の姫等がお祓いを受けにやってきている。
境内には、宇志丸宿禰が植えたのに始まるとされる「唐崎の松」がある。境内から琵琶湖を背景に唐崎の松を描いた[[歌川広重]]の「唐崎の夜雨」で知られており、[[近江八景]]に選ばれている。宇志丸宿禰が植えた初代の松は[[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）に大風で倒れ、天正19年（[[1591年]]）、[[大津城]]主だった[[新庄直頼]]の弟・[[新庄直忠]]が2代目の松を植えた。2代目も[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）に枯れ、現在のものは3代目である。[[金沢市]]の[[兼六園]]にある唐崎の松はこの地から分けられたものである。
古くから、和歌や俳句に取り上げられている。 {{Quotation|唐崎の 松は扇の 要にて こぎ行く船は 墨絵なりけり|紀貫之（古今和歌集）}} {{Quotation|唐崎の 松は花より 朧にて|松尾芭蕉}}
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|境内の松 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|境内の松 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|対岸の三上山 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|唐崎夜雨の石碑と File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|霊松鳥居から File:近江八景の碑.jpg|近江八景の碑 </gallery>
== 文化財 == === 滋賀県指定文化財 === * 名勝 ** 唐崎（唐崎神社境内） - 1998年（平成10年）6月19日指定<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf 県指定史跡・名勝・天然記念物]}}（滋賀県ホームページ）。</ref>。
=== 大津市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 唐崎（唐崎神社境内地） - 1981年（昭和56年）1月16日指定（史跡・名勝）<ref>昭和56年1月16日大津市教育委員会告示第3号。</ref><ref>境内説明板。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日吉大社]] * [[近江八景]]
== 外部リンク == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=唐崎神社}} - 日吉大社ホームページ内
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からさきしんしや}} [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:日吉大社]] [[Category:琵琶湖]] [[Category:摂末社]]

revision.comment: /* 由緒 */ [[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#丸括弧・波括弧・角括弧]]「原則として括弧の種類は本文の文字種に合わせます（例：本文が日本語の場合は全角括弧を用います）」に基づき、本文が日本語の(『日吉社神道秘密記』)の半角括弧を全角に修正。[[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#西暦・和暦]]「（新暦採用後の1873年〈明治6年〉1月1日以後）原則として西暦を先に表記します」に基づき、「大正10年（1921年）」→「1921年（大正10年）」に修正。他。

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |Address = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. ## Enshrined Deity The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). ## History According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), **Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune** (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous **Karasaki Pine** (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the **Eight Views of Ōmi** (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |Address = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. ## Enshrined Deity The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). ## History According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), **Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune** (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous **Karasaki Pine** (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the **Eight Views of Ōmi** (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} ## Gallery File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* ## Cultural Properties ### Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties * **Scenic Site** * **Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)** – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). ### Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties * **Historic Site** * **Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)** – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. ## References {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} ## Related Topics * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] ## External Links {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Karasaki Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Karasaki Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Karasaki Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Karasaki Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[en:Karasaki Shrine]][[en:Karasaki Shrine]][[ja:唐崎神社]][[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karasaki Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |Address = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. ## Enshrined Deity The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). ## History According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), **Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune** (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous **Karasaki Pine** (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the **Eight Views of Ōmi** (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} ## Gallery File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* ## {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |Address = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. ## Enshrined Deity The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). ## History According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), **Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune** (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous **Karasaki Pine** (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the **Eight Views of Ōmi** (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} ## Gallery File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* ## {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |Address = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. ## Enshrined Deity The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). ## History According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), **Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune** (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous **Karasaki Pine** (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the **Eight Views of Ōmi** (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} ## Gallery File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |Address = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. ## Enshrined Deity The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). ## History According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), **Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune** (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous **Karasaki Pine** (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the **Eight Views of Ōmi** (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} ## Gallery File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |Address = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |Address = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |Address = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' * '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |Address = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' * '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha Shrines]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}}

revision.comment: 唐崎の松についての記述の追加。「唐崎の松は扇の要にてこぎ行く船は墨絵なりけり」の和歌は『古今和歌集』には掲載されておらず、紀貫之とする確かな証拠もないため、唐崎の松を詠んだ勅撰和歌集の和歌として、藤原為家の歌に差し替えた。
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|大津市の唐崎神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 唐崎神社 |画像=[[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[大津市]][[唐崎 (大津市)|唐崎]]1-7-1 |位置 = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 女別当命 |社格 = 日吉大社摂社 |創建 = [[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = 春季大祭（[[4月28日]]）・秋季大祭（[[10月28日]]） |神事 = 御手洗祭（[[7月28日]]・[[7月29日|29日]]） }} '''唐崎神社'''（からさきじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]にある[[神社]]。[[日吉大社]]の[[摂社]]の一つである。[[琵琶湖]]西岸に位置し、境内は滋賀県指定名勝に指定されている。
== 祭神 == '''女別当命'''（わけすきひめのみこと）を祀る。
== 由緒 == [[日吉大社]]の社伝（『日吉社神道秘密記』<ref>日吉社[[禰宜]]であった[[祝部行丸]]（[[1512年|1512]]-[[1592年|1592]]）が記したもの。[[塙保己一]]編『[[群書類従]]』第二輯神祇部所収。</ref>）によれば、[[舒明天皇]]6年（[[633年]]）、{{Ruby|琴御館宇志丸宿禰|ことのみたちうしまる}}がこの地に居住し「唐崎」と名附けたといい、祭神はその宇志丸宿禰の妻とされる。当社は[[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]）に創建されたと伝えられ、かつては「女別当社」と呼ばれ、婦人病に霊験ありとして広く信仰を集めた。[[平安時代]]には「七瀬の祓」の一所として[[京都|京]]の人々や[[公家]]の姫等がお祓いを受けにやってきている。
== 唐崎の松 == 境内には、宇志丸宿禰が植えたのに始まるとされる「唐崎の松」がある<ref>唐崎の松を宇志丸宿禰が植えたとする由来も『日吉社神道秘密記』に記されたもの。</ref>。社伝以外での記載は、中世までくだるが、『[[太平記]]』や[[観阿弥]]作とされる[[謡曲]]『[[自然居士]]』にも「唐崎の一松」とみえる。また、和歌や俳句に取り上げられている。 {{Quotation|我みても昔は遠く成りにけり共に老木の唐崎の松|[[藤原為家|前大納言為家]]（『[[続拾遺和歌集]]』）}} {{Quotation|唐崎の松は花より朧にて|[[松尾芭蕉]]}}
[[近江八景]]「唐崎の夜雨」として選ばれ、それを絵画化した[[歌川広重]]の作品<ref>境内から琵琶湖を背景に唐崎の松を描いたもの。</ref>が知られている。宇志丸宿禰が植えた初代の松は[[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）に大風で倒れ、天正19年（[[1591年]]）、[[大津城]]主だった[[新庄直頼]]の弟・[[新庄直忠]]が2代目の松を植えた。2代目も[[1921年]]（[[大正]]10年）に枯れ、現在のものは3代目である。[[金沢市]]の[[兼六園]]にある唐崎の松はこの地から分けられたものである。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|境内の松 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|境内の松 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|対岸の三上山 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|唐崎夜雨の石碑と File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|霊松鳥居から File:近江八景の碑.jpg|近江八景の碑 </gallery>
== 文化財 == === 滋賀県指定文化財 === * 名勝 ** 唐崎（唐崎神社境内） - 1998年（平成10年）6月19日指定<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf 県指定史跡・名勝・天然記念物]}}（滋賀県ホームページ）。</ref>。
=== 大津市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 唐崎（唐崎神社境内地） - 1981年（昭和56年）1月16日指定（史跡・名勝）<ref>昭和56年1月16日大津市教育委員会告示第3号。</ref><ref>境内説明板。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日吉大社]] * [[近江八景]]
== 外部リンク == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=唐崎神社}} - 日吉大社ホームページ内
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からさきしんしや}} [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:日吉大社]] [[Category:琵琶湖]] [[Category:摂末社]]

revision.comment: /* 由緒 */ [[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#ハイフン]]「期間・区間・範囲をハイフンで表すときには、「 - 」（半角スペース＋ハイフン＋半角スペース）とします。 例：1990年 - 1999年」の「例：1990年 - 1999年」に基づき、「1512-1592」→「1512年 - 1592年」に修正。

revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha Shrines]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}}

as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha Shrines]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}}

A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha Shrines]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}}

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha Shrines]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}}

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan.
