The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha Shrines]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}}

revision.comment: /* 由緒 */ ルビの範囲を修正。また、唯一の出典によるものであり、その記述によるとして、伝聞調に修正した。
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|大津市の唐崎神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 唐崎神社 |画像=[[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[大津市]][[唐崎 (大津市)|唐崎]]1-7-1 |位置 = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 女別当命 |社格 = 日吉大社摂社 |創建 = [[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = 春季大祭（[[4月28日]]）・秋季大祭（[[10月28日]]） |神事 = 御手洗祭（[[7月28日]]・[[7月29日|29日]]） }} '''唐崎神社'''（からさきじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]にある[[神社]]。[[日吉大社]]の[[摂社]]の一つである。[[琵琶湖]]西岸に位置し、境内は滋賀県指定名勝に指定されている。
== 祭神 == '''女別当命'''（わけすきひめのみこと）を祀る。
== 由緒 == 以下は[[日吉大社]]の社伝（『日吉社神道秘密記』<ref>日吉社[[禰宜]]であった[[祝部行丸]]（[[1512年]] - [[1592年]]）が記したもの。[[塙保己一]]編『[[群書類従]]』第二輯神祇部所収。</ref>）による。[[舒明天皇]]6年（[[633年]]）、{{Ruby|琴御館|ことのみたち}}{{Ruby|宇志丸|うしまる}}宿禰がこの地に居住し「唐崎」と名附けたといい、祭神はその宇志丸宿禰の妻とされる。当社は[[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]）に創建されたと伝えられ、かつては「女別当社」と呼ばれ、婦人病に霊験ありとして広く信仰を集めたらしい。[[平安時代]]には「七瀬の祓」の一所として[[京都|京]]の人々や[[公家]]の姫等が参詣したという。
== 唐崎の松 == 境内には、宇志丸宿禰が植えたのに始まるとされる「唐崎の松」がある<ref>唐崎の松を宇志丸宿禰が植えたとする由来も『日吉社神道秘密記』に記されたもの。</ref>。社伝以外での記載は、中世までくだるが、『[[太平記]]』や[[観阿弥]]作とされる[[謡曲]]『[[自然居士]]』にも「唐崎の一松」とみえる。また、和歌や俳句に取り上げられている。 {{Quotation|我みても昔は遠く成りにけり共に老木の唐崎の松|[[藤原為家|前大納言為家]]（『[[続拾遺和歌集]]』）}} {{Quotation|唐崎の松は花より朧にて|[[松尾芭蕉]]}}
[[近江八景]]「唐崎の夜雨」として選ばれ、それを絵画化した[[歌川広重]]の作品<ref>境内から琵琶湖を背景に唐崎の松を描いたもの。</ref>が知られている。宇志丸宿禰が植えた初代の松は[[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）に大風で倒れ、天正19年（[[1591年]]）、[[大津城]]主だった[[新庄直頼]]の弟・[[新庄直忠]]が2代目の松を植えた。2代目も[[1921年]]（[[大正]]10年）に枯れ、現在のものは3代目である。[[金沢市]]の[[兼六園]]にある唐崎の松はこの地から分けられたものである。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|境内の松 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|境内の松 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|対岸の三上山 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|唐崎夜雨の石碑と File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|霊松鳥居から File:近江八景の碑.jpg|近江八景の碑 </gallery>
== 文化財 == === 滋賀県指定文化財 === * 名勝 ** 唐崎（唐崎神社境内） - 1998年（平成10年）6月19日指定<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf 県指定史跡・名勝・天然記念物]}}（滋賀県ホームページ）。</ref>。
=== 大津市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 唐崎（唐崎神社境内地） - 1981年（昭和56年）1月16日指定（史跡・名勝）<ref>昭和56年1月16日大津市教育委員会告示第3号。</ref><ref>境内説明板。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日吉大社]] * [[近江八景]]
== 外部リンク == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=唐崎神社}} - 日吉大社ホームページ内
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からさきしんしや}} [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:日吉大社]] [[Category:琵琶湖]] [[Category:摂末社]]

[[ファイル:Hiroshige Heavy rain on a pine tree 2.jpg|thumb|200px|[[歌川広重]]「唐崎夜雨」]] [[近江八景]]「唐崎の夜雨」として選ばれ、それを絵画化した[[歌川広重]]の作品<ref>境内から琵琶湖を背景に唐崎の松を描いたもの。</ref>が知られている。宇志丸宿禰が植えた初代の松は[[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）に大風で倒れ、天正19年（[[1591年]]）、[[大津城]]主だった[[新庄直頼]]の弟・[[新庄直忠]]が2代目の松を植えた。2代目も[[1921年]]（[[大正]]10年）に枯れ、現在のものは3代目である。[[金沢市]]の[[兼六園]]にある唐崎の松はこの地から分けられたものである。

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha Shrines]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}}

revision.comment: 56 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:唐崎神社 up to rev 104433923
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|大津市の唐崎神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 唐崎神社 |画像=[[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[大津市]][[唐崎 (大津市)|唐崎]]1-7-1 |位置 = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 女別当命 |社格 = 日吉大社摂社 |創建 = [[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = 春季大祭（[[4月28日]]）・秋季大祭（[[10月28日]]） |神事 = 御手洗祭（[[7月28日]]・[[7月29日|29日]]） }} '''唐崎神社'''（からさきじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]にある[[神社]]。[[日吉大社]]の[[摂社]]の一つである。[[琵琶湖]]西岸に位置し、境内は滋賀県指定名勝に指定されている。
== 祭神 == '''女別当命'''（わけすきひめのみこと）を祀る。
== 由緒 == 以下は[[日吉大社]]の社伝（『日吉社神道秘密記』<ref>日吉社[[禰宜]]であった[[祝部行丸]]（[[1512年]] - [[1592年]]）が記したもの。[[塙保己一]]編『[[群書類従]]』第二輯神祇部所収。</ref>）による。[[舒明天皇]]6年（[[633年]]）、{{Ruby|琴御館|ことのみたち}}{{Ruby|宇志丸|うしまる}}宿禰がこの地に居住し「唐崎」と名附けたといい、祭神はその宇志丸宿禰の妻とされる。当社は[[持統天皇]]11年（[[697年]]）に創建されたと伝えられ、かつては「女別当社」と呼ばれ、婦人病に霊験ありとして広く信仰を集めたらしい。[[平安時代]]には「七瀬の祓」の一所として[[京都|京]]の人々や[[公家]]の姫等が参詣したという。
== 唐崎の松 == 境内には、宇志丸宿禰が植えたのに始まるとされる「唐崎の松」がある<ref>唐崎の松を宇志丸宿禰が植えたとする由来も『日吉社神道秘密記』に記されたもの。</ref>。社伝以外での記載は、中世までくだるが、『[[太平記]]』や[[観阿弥]]作とされる[[謡曲]]『[[自然居士]]』にも「唐崎の一松」とみえる。また、和歌や俳句に取り上げられている。 {{Quotation|我みても昔は遠く成りにけり共に老木の唐崎の松|[[藤原為家|前大納言為家]]（『[[続拾遺和歌集]]』）}} {{Quotation|唐崎の松は花より朧にて|[[松尾芭蕉]]}}
[[ファイル:Hiroshige Heavy rain on a pine tree 2.jpg|thumb|200px|[[歌川広重]]「唐崎夜雨」]] [[近江八景]]「唐崎の夜雨」として選ばれ、それを絵画化した[[歌川広重]]の作品<ref>境内から琵琶湖を背景に唐崎の松を描いたもの。</ref>が知られている。宇志丸宿禰が植えた初代の松は[[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）に大風で倒れ、天正19年（[[1591年]]）、[[大津城]]主だった[[新庄直頼]]の弟・[[新庄直忠]]が2代目の松を植えた。2代目も[[1921年]]（[[大正]]10年）に枯れ、現在のものは3代目である。[[金沢市]]の[[兼六園]]にある唐崎の松はこの地から分けられたものである。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|境内の松 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|境内の松 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|対岸の三上山 File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|唐崎夜雨の石碑と File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|霊松鳥居から File:近江八景の碑.jpg|近江八景の碑 </gallery>
== 文化財 == === 滋賀県指定文化財 === * 名勝 ** 唐崎（唐崎神社境内） - 1998年（平成10年）6月19日指定<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf 県指定史跡・名勝・天然記念物]}}（滋賀県ホームページ）。</ref>。
=== 大津市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 唐崎（唐崎神社境内地） - 1981年（昭和56年）1月16日指定（史跡・名勝）<ref>昭和56年1月16日大津市教育委員会告示第3号。</ref><ref>境内説明板。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日吉大社]] * [[近江八景]]
== 外部リンク == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=唐崎神社}} - 日吉大社ホームページ内
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からさきしんしや}} [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:日吉大社]] [[Category:琵琶湖]] [[Category:摂末社]]

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha Shrines]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha Shrines]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasaki Shrine}}

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:大津市の神社]]

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Karasaki Shrine]] [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}}

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}}

{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], [[Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}}

more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}}

The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:Karasaki Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}}

more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}}

Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}}

more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.047389|N|135.87425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11418537]] Wikidata name: Karasaki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11418537]] Wikidata name: Karasaki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.047389|N|135.87425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11418537]] Wikidata name: Karasaki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11418537]] Wikidata name: Karasaki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]]. The shrine is situated on the western shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|[[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]] (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:唐崎神社]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|[[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]]}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] * [[:en:Eight Views of Ōmi|Eight Views of Ōmi]] == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐崎神社]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
