revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.047389|N|135.87425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11418537]] Wikidata name: Karasaki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11418537]] Wikidata name: Karasaki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}} |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}}. The shrine is situated on the western shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa}}, and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|{{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|WD=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki}} (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|{{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|WD=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō}}}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}} * {{ill|Eight Views of Ōmi|en|Eight Views of Ōmi|WD=Q340790|lt=Eight Views of Ōmi}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐崎神社]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|{{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|WD=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki}} (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|{{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|WD=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō}}}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}} * {{ill|Eight Views of Ōmi|en|Eight Views of Ōmi|WD=Q340790|lt=Eight Views of Ōmi}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐崎神社]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]]

The shrine is situated on the western shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa}}, and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|{{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|WD=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki}} (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—

The shrine is situated on the western shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}, and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|{{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|WD=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|{{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|WD=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō|1=Matsuo Bashō}}}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|{{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|WD=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō|1=Matsuo Bashō}}}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at Karasaki* File:Karasaki-jinja, matsu-1.jpg|Sacred pine viewed from the torii gate File:近江八景の碑.jpg|Monument for the *Eight Views of Ōmi* == Cultural Properties == === Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Scenic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on June 19, 1998 (Heisei 10).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaimokuroku/files/13.pdf Shiga Prefectural List of Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments]}} (Shiga Prefecture Official Website). === Ōtsu City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Karasaki (Karasaki Shrine Grounds)''' – Designated on January 16, 1981 (Shōwa 56) as a Historic Site and Scenic Spot.Ōtsu City Board of Education Notification No. 3, January 16, 1981.On-site explanatory board. == References == {{References Help}} {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} * {{ill|Eight Views of Ōmi|en|Eight Views of Ōmi|WD=Q340790|lt=Eight Views of Ōmi|1=Eight Views of Ōmi}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4796151838}} * {{Official website|http://hiyoshitaisha.jp/karasaki/|name=Karasaki Shrine}} – Official page within the Hiyoshi Taisha website {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|唐崎神社|version=104433923|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:唐崎神社]] {{Commons category|Karasaki-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11418537}} [[Category:Hiyoshi Taisha]] [[Category:Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Karasaki-jinja]][[ja:唐崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Otsu|qid=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.047389|N|135.87425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{nihongo|'''Karasaki Shrine'''|唐崎神社}} {{Otheruses|Karasaki Shrine in Ōtsu City}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine |Name = Karasaki Shrine |image = [[File:Karasaki-Shrine.jpg|250px]] {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=250|type=point}} |Location = {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|qid=Q202907|lt=Ōtsu|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, [[Japan]] |A11ress = Karasaki 1-7-1, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|02|50.6|N|135|52|27.3|E|region:JP_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Waketsukihime no Mikoto |Shrine Rank = Sessha of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} |Founded = 697 (Jitō 11) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Routes = |Annual Festival = Spring Grand Festival (April 28), Autumn Grand Festival (October 28) |Rituals = Mitarai Festival (July 28–29) }} '''Karasaki Shrine''' (唐崎神社, Karasaki Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|qid=Q202907|lt=Ōtsu|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the sessha (subsidiary shrines) of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}. The shrine is situated on the western shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|qid=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}, and its grounds have been designated a Prefectural Scenic Site of Shiga. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''Waketsukihime no Mikoto''' (女別当命). == History == According to the shrine records of Hiyoshi Taisha (*Hiyoshi Shrine Shintō Secret Records*), in the 6th year of Emperor Jomei (633 CE), '''Kotonomitachi Ushimaru Sukune''' (琴御館宇志丸宿禰) resided in this area and named it "Karasaki." The enshrined deity is said to be his wife. The shrine was founded in the 11th year of Emperor Jitō (697 CE) and was historically known as "Onna Bettōsha" (女別当社), attracting devotion for its reputed spiritual benefits for women's health. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was one of the *Shichinase Purification Sites* (七瀬の祓), where Kyoto nobles and princesses would come for purification rituals. Within the shrine grounds stands the famous '''Karasaki Pine''' (*Karasaki no Matsu*), which is said to have been planted by Ushimaru Sukune. The pine was depicted against the backdrop of Lake Biwa in *"Night Rain at Karasaki"* by [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]], one of the '''Eight Views of Ōmi''' (*Ōmi Hakkei*). The original tree was blown down in a storm in 1581 (Tenshō 9). A second-generation pine was planted in 1591 (Tenshō 19) by [[Shinjō Naotada]], the younger brother of [[Shinjō Naoyori]], the lord of [[Ōtsu Castle]]. The second tree perished in 1921 (Taishō 10), and the current pine is the third generation. The famous Karasaki Pine in [[Kenroku-en Garden]] in [[:en:Kanazawa City|Kanazawa City]] originated from a cutting taken from this tree. The shrine has been mentioned in many classical Japanese poems: {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki, the pivotal hinge of a folding fan—
as I row, the boat resembles an ink painting.|{{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|qid=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} (*Kokin Wakashū*)}} {{Quotation|The pine of Karasaki—
more hazy than the cherry blossoms.|{{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|qid=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō|1=Matsuo Bashō}}}} == Gallery == File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt1.jpg|Pine trees within the shrine grounds File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt2.jpg|Another view of the shrine's pine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt3.jpg|View of Mount Mikami from the shrine File:Karasaki-Shrine-nt4.jpg|Stone monument for *Night Rain at
